strengths: Most complete implementation of all 15 tasks: platform slopes with correct y-interpolation, mario walking/jumping/climbing, all 6 platforms with correct tilts (alternating slopes every platform), all 5 ladders connecting platforms with rungs rendered, broken ladders marked as cosmetic

Excellent barrel physics: spawns from DK every 2-4s, rolls at 120px/s along slopes, 20% ladder descent chance, falls with gravity 600px/s² to next platform, destroys on oil drum collision with flame animation

Sound design: complete audio suite with jump tone (330Hz triangle 50ms), death descending 440→55Hz (5-note falloff), smash noise burst, level complete 7-note arpeggio (300-1000Hz), hammer tick at 8Hz (660/880Hz toggle)

Collision detection: AABB for all entities, correct platform landing with feet-y tracking, barrel-mario contact kills (unless hammer active), jump-over scoring with barrel below logic, oil drum pickup

Hammer system: 2 hammers at correct positions, 8s duration, mario auto-swings with 100ms toggle, barrels destroyed on contact (+300pts), cannot climb ladders while holding hammer, visual blink in last 2s
Score system: +100 for jumping over barrel, +300 for hammer smash, +500 for level complete + time bonus (remaining time × 10), localStorage high score, HUD displays score/lives/level/bonus
State machine: IDLE → PLAYING → PAUSED → GAME_OVER with correct transitions. Sub-states: DYING (0.8s flash), LEVEL_COMPLETE (2s display then auto-advance)
Level progression: barrel interval -0.5s per level (min 1s), barrel roll speed +20px/s per level, bonus timer resets each level
Event code input handling: ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down/Space with correct event.code checks, prevents double-jump, debounces jump input

Visual polish: DK arm toss animation, Pauline HELP blinking at 2Hz, floating '+100' text for jump-overs with fade-out, bonus timer bar visual indicator (green→yellow→red), multiple render layers for platforms/ladders/entities
weaknesses: Platform edge detection uses +/−5px tolerance which can miss barrel-at-exact-edge cases, creating minor spawning inconsistency for ladder descent RNG
Ladder exit check at `mario.y <= lad.topY` doesn't verify mario actually landed on upper platform — could exit into void if upper platform doesn't exist
Barrel ladder descent uses proximity check (within 60px of edge) rather than spec's stricter centering requirement, allowing barrels far from ladder x to still take ladder down
DK throw animation shows tossing flag but arm angle visual not rendered — cosmetic only, no visual feedback of barrel spawn event
Bonus timer countdown uses interval accumulation (`bonusCountdown += dt`) which can drift under high frame-time variance; spec says "every 2 seconds" but implementation may fire at 1.99s or 2.01s
Mario walking animation uses 3-frame cycle but no visual sprites — only color changes, making walk less apparent visually

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable with core Donkey Kong elements and solid controls, but barrel/ladders logic, simplified visuals, and some rough collision/platform behaviors keep it from feeling fully arcade-accurate.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics are present but barrel physics and platform collision have issues—barrels often fall through platforms or behave erratically, ladder alignment with platforms is imprecise making climbing frustrating, and the jump-over-barrel scoring can trigger incorrectly, though the overall structure with start/pause/game-over/levels is solid.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally faithful recreation with working ladders, sloped platform physics, hammer power-ups, and a complete game loop including audio.

strengths: Core game loop with 60fps rendering and delta-time implemented correctly
Game states (IDLE/PLAYING/PAUSED/LEVEL_COMPLETE/GAME_OVER) defined and transitioning via Enter/P keys
6 platforms rendered as colored trapezoids with slopes (mostly correct y-interpolation using platform.y1 + t*(y2-y1))
Mario walks left/right at 150px/s, jumps with initial velocity -340px/s and gravity 900px/s²
Ladder array defined with 5 ladders + 3 broken ladders, stored as {x, topY, bottomY} structure
Barrels spawn from DK position, roll on platforms, attempt to fall/descend when reaching edges
Oil drum at (60,680) destroys barrels on contact with flame animation (orange rect flicker)
Hammer pickups placed at x=200 (platform 2) and x=400 (platform 4), collecting activates 8s timer
Lives system: starts with 3, decrements on barrel contact, game over at 0 lives
Score display, bonus timer display, localStorage high score persistence
weaknesses: **CRITICAL PHYSICS BUG**: Mario platform collision uses `getPlatformAtPos()` which checks `y >= platformY - 2 && y <= platformY + 8` with a fixed 2px top margin and 8px bottom — this creates incorrect platform heights and allows mario to sink into platforms or float above them. Spec requires mario to stand on the EXACT platform surface

**Mario Y-tracking breaks on slopes**: getYOnPlatform() calculates platform y-intercept correctly, but mario.y after collision is set to getYOnPlatform() value without subtracting mario.height — mario should be 32px ABOVE the slope line, not at it. This causes mario to sink into the platform or appear floating
**Platform edge detection missing**: Mario can walk off platform edges without transition logic, no check for clamping x to platform.x1/x2 bounds
**Ladder entry tolerance too loose**: `Math.abs(x - ladders[i].x) < 8` checks ladder x-center proximity but doesn't verify mario is near a ladder's y-range. Can enter ladder from far below or above

**Ladder exit logic broken**: Ladders stored with topY=100 to bottomY=710 (full-screen range), but mario.y clamping on exit doesn't respect actual ladder bounds. Mario can climb full height even on broken ladders marked as non-functional

**Barrel physics oversimplified**: Barrels roll at constant 120px/s regardless of platform slope direction (spec says 'direction alternates per platform'). Barrels don't respect slope y-interpolation — just roll horizontally at fixed height
**Jump-over scoring not implemented**: No tracking of barrel below mario during jump, no floating '+100' text, jump-over mechanic appears non-functional
**Hammer swing animation missing**: `mario.hammerActive` flag set but no visual representation of swinging hammer or barrel destruction animation
**Platform ladder connections incorrect**: Ladders defined as full-height (y=100 to y=710) rather than connecting specific platform pairs. No validation that ladder endpoints match platform edges
**Visual fidelity: simple rectangles**: Mario is plain red rectangle, barrels are plain brown ellipses, no detail. Platforms are uncolored or same color. No distinction between different entity types
**No audio**: No Web Audio API usage, completely silent

**Collision detection: AABB only, no slope consideration**: Barrels and mario don't account for platform slopes when checking collision — treats all platforms as flat, causing barrels to phase through sloped sections
**Donkey Kong and Pauline rendering minimal**: DK is simple brown rectangle without any animation, Pauline is pink rectangle without HELP text animation
**Bonus timer death condition missing**: Bonus reaches 0 but mario doesn't die — no game-over trigger on bonus expiry
**No floating text for scoring events**: No '+100' display for jump-overs, no '+300' for hammer smashes, no '+500' for level complete with time bonus calculation

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable at a basic level with movement, ladders, barrels, scoring, lives, and restart, but the stage layout, barrel/platform logic, climbing rules, jump-over scoring, and overall resemblance to original Donkey Kong are notably inaccurate.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Barrel physics are broken - they don't properly roll down sloped platforms or transition between levels, Mario's platform collision is unreliable causing falling through floors, climbing mechanics are buggy since ladder positions don't align well with platforms, and jumping over barrels for points rarely works due to flawed collision logic.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: A solid recreation of the first level with working ladders and barrel physics, though the barrel falling logic is a bit simplistic compared to the original.

strengths: Complete 15-task implementation with all core mechanics: platforms, ladders, mario movement, barrels, hammers, scoring
Strong keyboard input handling: event.code for layout-independent input (ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down/Space/Enter/P), event.repeat rejection to prevent key-holding drift
Platform physics correctly implemented: 6 platforms with slope interpolation using `platformYAt()` function, mario y-tracking follows slopes with `feetX = mario.x + MARIO_W/2` center calculation

Barrel mechanics: spawns from DK every 2-4s (random delay), rolls at speed proportional to level (`120 + (level-1)*20`), 20% ladder descent chance with correct direction determination (platform.id % 2 === 1 ? 1 : -1)
Correct barrel fall physics: gravity 600px/s², lands on platform with `platformYAt()` recalculation, smooth transition from roll→fall→roll states
Jump-over scoring implemented: tracks `scoredJumpIds` per barrel per jump, awards +100 when barrel passes beneath mario during jump arc with floating text display
Hammer system: 2 pickups at spec positions, 8s duration, mario auto-swings with animation counter (`mario.hammerAnim += dt*10`), barrels destroyed on contact (+300pts), visual blink in last 2s
Sound design: complete audio with jump (330Hz triangle), smash (720→280Hz), death (440→55Hz over 500ms), level complete (2-note arpeggio), hammer tick (660/880Hz toggle)
Correct bonus timer system: -100 every 2s, visual bar shrinks from right side, 0 bonus triggers death via `killMario()`
Level progression: barrel spawn interval decreases per level (`Math.max(1, 2 - (level-1)*0.5)`), roll speed increases per level (`120 + (level-1)*20`), bonus resets each level
Visual polish: Mario drawn as rectangle with separate head/body colors, barrels rendered as ellipses with horizontal band, platforms as trapezoids, ladders with rungs, oil drum with animated flames
HUD displays: Score, best score (localStorage), lives as icon/count, level number, bonus timer with visual bar, floating damage numbers for scoring events
State machine with sub-states: STATES (IDLE/PLAYING/PAUSED/GAME_OVER) + SUB (ACTIVE/DYING/LEVEL_COMPLETE) allows fine-grained game flow control
Correct start screen with 'DONKEY KONG' title, game-over screen with overlay, level-complete message
weaknesses: **Ladder exit bounds check fragile**: Uses `usable = ladderAvailable(ladder)` and checks `mario.y + MARIO_H <= usable.topY + 4` to detect platform exit, but doesn't verify mario actually landed on the upper platform surface — could exit into void or miss platform by 4px
**Barrel ladder descent detection uses proximity heuristic**: `Math.abs(l.x - centerX) < 18` allows barrels far from ladder to still descend, not strict centering check
**Platform edge detection for mario**: Uses `clamp(mario.x, minX, maxX)` which hard-clamps mario to platform bounds without smooth edge behavior or transition zones

**Broken ladder implementation**: Breaks ladders by limiting `bottomY` but doesn't prevent mario from climbing broken section — mario can still enter and climb full length if starts in non-broken zone

**Mario respawn after death doesn't reset on platform surface**: `resetPositions()` sets `mario.y = platformYAt(...)` correctly, but doesn't set `mario.onGround = true`, causing 1-frame fall/interpolation jitter on respawn
**Bonus timer accumulation jitter**: Uses `bonusTick += dt` which can accumulate drift; threshold check `bonusTick >= 2` fires inconsistently if frame times vary
**DK toss animation only shows timer**: `dk.tossAnim` countdown doesn't render visual arm movement or indication that barrel spawned
**Pauline animation minimal**: HELP text appears but no head/eyes/pose variation during blink
**Walk animation for mario missing visual feedback**: No sprite sheet or frame animation, just positional movement
**Ladder rung rendering inconsistent**: Ladders drawn with rungs every fixed y-interval, but doesn't scale rung spacing per ladder height

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable with movement, jumping, ladders, barrels, scoring, lives, pause, and restart, but the level layout/behavior is only an approximation of arcade Donkey Kong and some ladder/barrel logic appears simplified and slightly inconsistent.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics of platforming, barrel dodging, ladder climbing, hammers, and level progression are implemented, though barrel pathfinding on sloped platforms can be inconsistent and the platform layout doesn't perfectly match the original arcade's iconic tilted girder arrangement.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented clone featuring accurate sloped platform physics, ladder mechanics, hammer power-ups, and even a basic audio engine.

strengths: Game runs without console errors
Basic input handling with arrow keys and Space for jump
Simple collision detection between player and barrels with game-over trigger
Platforms render and player can walk on them
Barrel spawning system with timer
Score increments on barrel removal
weaknesses: **WRONG CANVAS SIZE**: 640×480 instead of spec's 700×800 — 13% narrower, 40% shorter. Completely fails T01 canvas requirement
**NOT DONKEY KONG**: Missing 90% of spec requirements. This is a minimal platformer tech demo, not the iconic DK gameplay

**Missing core mechanics**: No sloped platforms (all platforms are flat horizontal rectangles), no ladders, no climbing, no Donkey Kong character, no Pauline, no barrel rolling on slopes, no ladder descent for barrels

**Platform physics broken**: Platforms are simple rectangles at fixed heights, no slope interpolation, no y-variation per x-position. Barrels roll horizontally at constant height regardless of platform
**Barrels don't roll**: Barrels have simple `dx` velocity, no slope-following physics. Direction doesn't alternate per platform (spec requires this)
**No jump-over scoring**: No +100 points for jumping over barrels, no floating text display
**No hammer power-up**: No hammer pickups, no barrel destruction, no 8s duration, no auto-swing animation
**No bonus timer**: No 'BONUS: {N}' display, no -100 every 2 seconds, no death-on-zero-bonus mechanic
**No level progression**: Single static difficulty, no speed increases per level, no ladder descent or complex barrel behavior
**No proper game states**: Game starts immediately, no IDLE screen, no PAUSED state, no proper GAME_OVER with restart (only sets `gameOver` flag, no restart logic)
**Collision detection primitive**: Simple `rectIntersect()` for all entities, no slope-aware collision, no distinction between Mario feet and barrel center
**Visual fidelity minimal**: Mario is yellow rectangle, Kong is brown square, no animation, no detail. Barrels are orange squares, not barrels
**No audio**: Completely silent, no Web Audio API
**Donkey Kong static/no animation**: Kong doesn't throw barrels — barrels spawn from kong position but no animation feedback
**No Pauline/win condition**: No win state for reaching Pauline, no STAGE CLEAR screen, no level completion
**Canvas height only 480 instead of 800**: All vertical positioning off by 40%, platforms stacked too close
**Limited to 4 platforms**: Spec requires 6 platforms with specific slopes. Only 4 static flat platforms
**Barrels spawn in same area repeatedly**: No variation in spawn pattern, predictable barrel behavior
**Death animation**: `gameOver = true` sets flag but no restart mechanism — game halts, no Enter-to-restart
**Score system trivial**: +10 per barrel removal, no +100 jump-over, +300 hammer smash, +500 level complete bonuses

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable as a very simplified platform dodger, but it lacks ladders, sloped girder layout, rescue goal, proper Donkey Kong presentation, and restart/win-state flow.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Bare-bones implementation with basic movement, gravity, and barrel spawning, but lacks the iconic sloped girders, ladders, Pauline rescue goal, proper level layout, restart mechanism, and looks nothing like Donkey Kong; canvas is 640x480 instead of 700x800, platforms are flat and sparse, and there is no win condition.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: While technically a platformer with barrels, it lacks the iconic sloped girders, ladders, and Pauline, making it a very generic and simplified version of the requested game.

opus.notes: Persistent network failures on complex games — likely context limit

rankings.summary: Best overall implementation. Complete 15-task checklist with excellent barrel physics, sound design (7-note level jingle), all platform slopes with correct interpolation, proper hammer mechanics, jump-over scoring with floating text. Perfect input handling with event.code and debouncing. Only minor issues: bonus timer drift, ladder exit validation, arm animation cosmetic.

Strong secondary implementation. Excellent input handling with event.repeat rejection, correct barrel ladder-descent RNG (20% chance with proximity check), level progression with difficulty scaling, proper bonus timer with bar indicator, jump-over scoring with floating text. Minor weaknesses: ladder exit bounds check loose, barrel spawn animation missing, broken ladder climbing not fully prevented.

Incomplete implementation with critical physics bugs. Platform collision broken (fixed 2px margin causes mario to sink/float), mario y-position doesn't account for height above slope, barrel physics oversimplified (ignores slopes, doesn't alternate direction), jump-over scoring non-functional, hammer animation missing, bonus death condition missing. No audio. Visual fidelity poor (rectangles only).

Not Donkey Kong. Wrong canvas (640×480 instead of 700×800), no slopes, no ladders, no climbing, no Pauline, no hammers, no bonus timer, no proper game states. Single flat-platform tech demo missing 90% of spec. Canvas too small, barrels don't roll on slopes, level progression absent, game-over lacks restart mechanism. Completely fails benchmark requirements.
